skill.name: routeros-container
skill.description: RouterOS /container subsystem for running OCI containers on MikroTik devices. Use when: enabling containers on RouterOS, setting up VETH/bridge networking for containers, managing container lifecycle via CLI or REST API, building OCI images for RouterOS, configuring container environment variables, troubleshooting container issues, or when the user mentions RouterOS container, /container, VETH, device-mode container, or MikroTik Docker.
skill.summary: Manage OCI containers on MikroTik RouterOS devices with networking, lifecycle, and image configuration.
skill.author: tikoci

security_audit.summary: This skill is a Markdown documentation file providing instructions and examples for the MikroTik RouterOS /container subsystem. The static scanner flagged 171 patterns, but all are false positives. The 'external_commands' detections match RouterOS CLI syntax inside Markdown code fences, not actual shell execution. The 'network' detections reference example URLs and IPs in documentation snippets. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' and 'path traversal' findings have no basis in the content. No executable code exists in this file - it is purely instructional documentation for network administrators.

content.value_statement: Running containers on MikroTik RouterOS requires understanding its custom OCI implementation, which differs significantly from standard Docker. This skill provides step-by-step guidance for container setup, networking, image management, and lifecycle operations on RouterOS devices.
actual_capabilities: Guide device-mode activation for container support on RouterOS 7.x
Configure VETH interfaces and bridge networking for containers
Set up NAT and firewall rules for container traffic
Import local tar images or pull from Docker registries
Manage container lifecycle via CLI commands or REST API
Configure environment variables and volume mounts with inline or named patterns
limitations: Only applies to RouterOS 7.x with the container extra package installed
Does not support MIPS architecture for containers
Device-mode activation requires physical access to the router hardware
Local tar images must be single-layer, uncompressed, and use Docker v1 manifest format

use_cases.title: Deploy Pi-hole in a RouterOS container
use_cases.description: Set up a Pi-hole ad-blocking container with VETH networking, persistent storage on external disk, and proper environment variables for the web interface password.

use_cases.title: Run netinstall service via L2 bridge container
use_cases.description: Configure a container with Layer 2 bridge access to provide BOOTP/TFTP netinstall services on the same network segment as physical devices.

use_cases.title: Automate container management via REST API
use_cases.description: Use the RouterOS REST API to programmatically list, start, stop, and delete containers with proper polling for asynchronous operations.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner: Enable containers on my router
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a MikroTik router running RouterOS 7.x. Help me enable the container subsystem and set up basic VETH networking so I can run my first container.

prompt_templates.title: Intermediate: Pull and run a container from Docker Hub
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to pull an Alpine container from Docker Hub on my RouterOS device, assign it a VETH interface with IP 172.17.0.2, and set environment variables. Walk me through the steps.
prompt_templates.scenario: Registry pull with networking and configuration

prompt_templates.title: Advanced: Build and import a custom single-layer image
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to build a custom OCI image for RouterOS that meets its requirements: single layer, no gzip compression, Docker v1 manifest format. Then import it as a tar file and configure inline env vars and mounts.

prompt_templates.title: Expert: REST API container lifecycle automation
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a TypeScript script that uses the RouterOS REST API to list containers, start one by ID, check its running status (accounting for string boolean values), and delete it with proper retry logic for the stop-then-delete sequence.
prompt_templates.scenario: Programmatic container management via REST API

output_examples.input: Create a VETH interface and bridge for a container with IP 172.17.0.2
output_examples.output:
1. Create VETH pair: /interface/veth/add name=veth-myapp address=172.17.0.2/24 gateway=172.17.0.1
2. Create bridge: /interface/bridge/add name=containers
3. Add VETH to bridge: /interface/bridge/port/add bridge=containers interface=veth-myapp
4. Assign gateway IP: /ip/address/add address=172.17.0.1/24 interface=containers

output_examples.input: Start a container named myapp and check its status
output_examples.output:
1. Start: /container/start [find tag~"myapp"]
2. Check status: /container/print
3. View logs: /log/print where topics~"container"
4. For REST API: GET /rest/container returns container list with .running field (string "true"/"false")

best_practices: Always place container volumes on external disk storage, never on internal flash storage
Use inline env= and mount= properties on RouterOS 7.21+ for simpler self-contained container definitions
When using the REST API, remember that the .running field returns strings "true"/"false", not boolean values, and DELETE requires the container to be fully stopped first
anti_patterns: Using /system/reboot instead of /system/package/apply-changes on RouterOS 7.18+ which will discard uploaded packages
Attempting to run multi-layer or gzip-compressed tar images which RouterOS cannot load
Trying to use the /app YAML system when Layer 2 bridge access is required, such as for netinstall

faq.question: What RouterOS version do I need for container support?
faq.answer: RouterOS 7.x with the container extra package installed. The container feature varies by version, with significant changes between 7.20 and 7.21 for environment variables and mount syntax.

faq.question: Why does RouterOS need device-mode for containers?
faq.answer: Device-mode is a RouterOS security feature that gates multiple features including containers. It requires physical confirmation via reset button or power cycle to prevent remote activation.

faq.question: Can I use standard Docker images on RouterOS?
faq.answer: Registry pulls from Docker Hub work directly. Local tar imports must be single-layer, uncompressed, and use Docker v1 manifest format. Multi-layer and gzip-compressed images are not supported.

faq.question: What is the difference between inline and named env/mount configuration?
faq.answer: Inline (env= and mount= on /container/add) is the modern 7.21+ approach that keeps containers self-contained. Named lists require separate /container/envs/add and /container/mounts/add objects and work across more versions.

faq.question: How do I manage containers programmatically?
faq.answer: Use the RouterOS REST API at /rest/container. Key endpoints include GET for listing, POST to /container/start or /container/stop, and DELETE to remove. The .running field is a string, not a boolean.

faq.question: When should I use /app YAML versus manual container setup?
faq.answer: Use /app YAML (RouterOS 7.22+) for standard deployments with port forwarding. Use manual VETH/bridge setup when you need Layer 2 access, such as for netinstall, DHCP relay, or direct network access.
